definitions: A search for and persecution of people accused of witchcraft.
A campaign directed against a person or group accused of disloyalty, subversion, or some other real or imagined crime, typically with the aim of undermining or discrediting them.
examples: The senator called the investigation a political witch hunt.
Historically, witch hunts led to many innocent deaths.
Critics described the media frenzy as a modern-day witch hunt.
